topic.what: Metabolomics profiling study characterizing the systemic metabolic response to electroacupuncture (EA) treatment in a chronic atrophic gastritis (CAG) animal model, with identification of specific biomarker metabolites.
topic.how: CAG rat model treated with EA at ST36 and CV12; serum metabolome profiled by GC-MS or LC-MS; pathway enrichment and biomarker discovery using univariate/multivariate statistics; comparison to untreated CAG and healthy controls.
topic.why_matters: Provides a molecular fingerprint of EA action in CAG — transforming an empirical treatment into a set of measurable, hypothesis-generating metabolic endpoints that can anchor future mechanistic and clinical research.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number and specificity of metabolites significantly altered by EA vs. CAG model group
characteristics.value: Multiple differential metabolites identified, enriched in amino acid and energy metabolism pathways
characteristics.evidence: VIP scores and fold-change thresholds from PLS-DA
characteristics.context: Rat serum metabolome; human translation not directly tested

characteristics.what_it_measures: Metabolic pathways most perturbed by EA treatment relative to disease state
characteristics.value: Amino acid metabolism, TCA cycle, and lipid metabolism pathways enriched
characteristics.context: Pathway-level interpretation; individual enzyme targets not identified

good_for: Researchers building metabolomic panels for EA treatment monitoring in gastric disease
Studies seeking to link EA-induced neural signals to downstream systemic metabolic changes
Biomarker discovery for non-invasive monitoring of CAG treatment response
not_for: Clinical biomarker use prior to human validation studies
Mechanistic studies requiring protein-level or genomic resolution
requires: Rat CAG model (MNNG or iodoacetamide induction)

novelty: Among the earliest studies applying untargeted metabolomics specifically to EA treatment response in a CAG model
Identifies energy and amino acid metabolism as primary systemic mediators of EA effect — distinct from purely local gastric mucosal explanations
open_problems: Causal relationship between identified metabolites and gastric mucosal repair not established
Specificity of metabolic signature to EA vs. other acupoint stimulation modalities not tested in this study
Human serum metabolomics validation cohort needed
